examples.evidence: idx 10,11: instruction asks <70 but model issues 'price<50' and the page state shows 'lower than 50.00 dollars'.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the model's search queries or referenced instruction values diverge from the original instruction (e.g., changing the price limit or swapping men/women).
active_rubrics.title: Query drift / altered instruction parameters

active_rubrics.description: Model repeats the exact same action many times in a row with no behavioral change despite identical observations.

examples.evidence: idx 4–7: 'search[...]' followed by 'click[back to search]' repeated identically across the whole trajectory.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the model issues the same action 3+ consecutive times without any change in strategy.
active_rubrics.title: Repetitive non-progressing actions

active_rubrics.description: Model repeatedly issues search queries and clicks 'back to search' instead of clicking on returned product ASINs, never reaching item pages or purchasing.

examples.evidence: All 12 rollouts: after results like 'b09s3bn15c, ...' the model clicks 'back to search' instead of opening a product.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the trajectory shows the model returning to search or re-searching repeatedly without ever clicking a product ASIN from the search results.
active_rubrics.title: Search-loop without clicking products

active_rubrics.description: Model generates its own 'Observation:' and 'Page state:' content inside the action turn, hallucinating environment responses rather than waiting for the real tool output.

examples.evidence: idx 0–11 all include 'Observation:' and 'Page state:' text inside the model response after each action.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the model's response contains self-written 'Observation:' or 'Page state:' blocks following its action (faking tool output).
active_rubrics.title: Fabricated/hallucinated observations

active_rubrics.description: Model never issues click[buy now] or selects required options (size/color), failing to advance toward task completion.

examples.evidence: All 12 rollouts terminate while still on search/results pages with no product opened or purchased.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the trajectory ends with no product click, no option selection, and no buy action.

active_rubrics.description: Model repeatedly searches, then clicks 'back to search', then re-issues the same search, never clicking a product result. It wastes the entire trajectory in an unproductive loop and never reaches a buy step.

examples.evidence: Idx 0,1,2,3,8,9,10,11 all repeat search[...] then click[back to search] then search again with no product clicks.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the trajectory contains 2+ cycles of search -> back to search -> same/similar search without ever opening (clicking) a product detail page.
active_rubrics.title: Search-then-back-to-search loop

active_rubrics.description: Model buys the first product it clicks without checking required attributes (color, size, price). In idx 5, it bought a $100 unisex t-shirt despite price<70 and 3x-large/cotton candy requirements.

examples.evidence: Idx 5: clicks b09qqjj3km (priced $100, t-shirt, no options selected) then immediately 'buy now', getting reward 0.428.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the model clicks 'buy now' without selecting required options (size/color) or when the product price/title clearly violates the instruction's required attributes.
active_rubrics.title: Premature purchase without attribute verification

active_rubrics.description: After receiving a list of product ASINs, the model clicks 'back to search' instead of clicking any product to inspect attributes, preventing any chance of fulfilling the task.

examples.evidence: Idx 0,1,2,3,6,7,8,9,10,11: results returned with ASINs (e.g., b003i567w4) but model only clicks 'back to search'.

active_rubrics.rubric: Score 1 if the trajectory shows search results with product ASIN clickables but the model never clicks any product ASIN.
active_rubrics.title: Failure to click product results
